task_completion.visual_evidence: The Odoo Expenses app is open, showing the dashboard or list of expenses.

task_completion.description: Locate the target expense report in the list
task_completion.visual_evidence: The list view shows the 'Q4 Digital Marketing Conference' report, possibly with a search filter applied.

task_completion.description: Open the detailed view of the expense report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The detailed form view of the 'Q4 Digital Marketing Conference' expense report is open, showing 'Eli Lambert' as the employee.

task_completion.description: Verify the expense items and total amount
task_completion.visual_evidence: The expense lines are visible showing Conference Registration, Hotel Accommodation, and Meals, with a total of $925.00.
The status indicator on the top right of the report shows 'Approved', and the 'Approve' button is no longer visible.

integrity.description: Agent uses the web browser to interact with Odoo
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Odoo web interface in a browser, not a text editor or API client.

integrity.description: Agent does not use terminal commands to approve the report
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are used to execute python scripts or psql commands to modify the database directly.

integrity.description: The state change to 'Approved' is genuine
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI consistently reflects the 'Approved' state (e.g., status bar updates, 'Approve' button disappears, 'Post Journal Entries' button appears), rather than just text edited via Inspect Element.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Employee: Eli Lambert. Report Title: 'Q4 Digital Marketing Conference'. Expected Total: $925.00 (Conference Registration: $350.00, Hotel Accommodation: $480.00, Meals: $95.00). The report starts in the 'Submitted' state. The agent must click 'Approve' to change the state to 'Approved'. In Odoo 17, the status is shown in the top right corner of the form view.
